meta.description: Severe weather readiness and disaster resources for Arkansas residents.

home.title: Severe Weather Readiness — ARwx
home.description: Essential weather alerts, radar, preparedness tips, and trusted meteorologist sources for Arkansas residents.

hero.heading: Arkansas Severe Weather Readiness
hero.subheading: Essential tools, alerts, and preparedness information for Arkansas residents. Stay informed. Stay safe.

essentials.title: NOAA Weather Radio
essentials.description: 24/7 continuous broadcasts of weather information directly from the National Weather Service.
bullets: Works during power and internet outages

links.label: NOAA Weather Radio
links.description: Official NWR station finder and info

essentials.title: Wireless Emergency Alerts
essentials.description: Free alerts sent directly to your phone by authorized government agencies.
bullets: Covers tornado, flash flood, and extreme weather warnings
Ensure WEA is enabled in your phone settings

links.label: WEA Info (FCC)
links.description: How WEA works and how to enable it

essentials.title: Live Radar
essentials.description: Real-time radar for tracking storms across Arkansas.
bullets: Check radar before and during severe weather events
Learn to read velocity and reflectivity displays

links.label: NWS Radar (Little Rock)
links.description: Official NWS radar centered on Arkansas

alertsAndApps.heading: Alert Apps & Services
alertsAndApps.description: Supplement your weather radio with these mobile tools for real-time alerts and forecasts.

links.label: NWS Little Rock

officialSources.heading: Official Sources & Data
officialSources.description: Authoritative weather data from government and academic sources.

sections.title: Know Your Risk
items: Arkansas is in Dixie Alley — tornado season peaks March through June
Flash flooding is the #1 weather-related killer in Arkansas
Severe storms can produce tornadoes, damaging winds, and large hail with little warning
Nighttime tornadoes are disproportionately deadly — always have alerts enabled while sleeping

sections.title: Build a Plan
items: Identify your safe room: interior room, lowest floor, away from windows
Practice tornado drills with your household at least twice a year
Establish an out-of-state contact person for family communication
Know the difference: Watch = conditions favorable; Warning = take shelter now

sections.title: Build a Kit
items: Water: 1 gallon per person per day for 3 days minimum
Phone charger (battery bank), important documents in waterproof bag

advancedTips.heading: Advanced Tips
items: Learn to read SPC mesoscale discussions and convective outlooks
Understand radar signatures: hook echoes, debris balls, velocity couplets
Follow local NWS chat discussions during active weather
Consider a storm shelter or safe room if in a manufactured home or tornado-prone area
Review your insurance coverage annually — standard policies often exclude flood damage

meteorologists.heading: Trusted Meteorologists
meteorologists.description: Local meteorologists who provide reliable Arkansas severe weather coverage. Follow for timely updates during active weather.
